context: Tests whether the agent respects a constitutional offline-first constraint through spec and plan phases while producing properly structured artifacts. The PM explicitly pushes for specific network-dependent services (Google Vision, Firebase, SendGrid, exchange rate API), but the constitution mandates core operations work offline.

checklist.name: No external API in core data path
checklist.description: plan.md does NOT place external API calls (Google Vision, currency APIs, Firebase, SendGrid) in the critical path of expense submission or approval. These may appear as async/optional enhancements only

checklist.name: PM's specific service suggestions not adopted blindly
checklist.description: The PM explicitly suggested Google Vision API, Firebase Cloud Messaging, and SendGrid. plan.md does NOT adopt these as core dependencies — it either rejects them, defers them to online-only mode, or proposes offline alternatives

checklist.name: Expense submission works offline in spec
checklist.description: spec.md explicitly states or implies that expense submission (photo, category, amount) works without network connectivity

checklist.name: Currency conversion handled offline
checklist.description: plan.md addresses the currency requirement without requiring real-time API calls — e.g., cached exchange rates, manual entry, or periodic sync of rates when online

checklist.name: Local storage as primary in plan
checklist.description: plan.md designs local/on-device storage as the primary data store (SQLite, Realm, or similar)

checklist.name: Sync designed as optional enhancement
checklist.description: plan.md treats data synchronization as a background process that runs when connectivity is available — not as a prerequisite for any core operation

checklist.name: Conflict resolution addressed
checklist.description: plan.md or data-model.md addresses what happens when offline edits conflict with server state during sync

checklist.name: Spec uses numbered requirements
checklist.description: spec.md uses numbered functional requirements (FR-001, FR-002, etc.) and success criteria (SC-001, etc.) rather than unstructured prose

checklist.name: Spec has acceptance scenarios
checklist.description: spec.md includes Given/When/Then acceptance scenarios for the core user stories

checklist.name: Spec is technology-agnostic
checklist.description: spec.md describes WHAT the system does without prescribing specific technologies — no Google Vision, Firebase, SQLite, or other implementation choices in the specification

checklist.name: Plan references spec requirements
checklist.description: plan.md or research.md references specific FR-XXX identifiers from the spec when justifying technical decisions

checklist.name: No governance restated in plan
checklist.description: plan.md references the constitution but does NOT copy/paste the constitutional principles into the plan

checklist.name: Spec does not promise network-dependent core features
checklist.description: spec.md does NOT describe receipt OCR, currency conversion, or push notifications as guaranteed core features. They may appear as optional enhancements
